title: tanacetum gmelinii
intro: Tanacetum gmelinii, also known as Mongolian tansy, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Mongolia and China. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to repel insects and worms due to its pyrethrum content. The plant has been found to have various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions. Tanacetum gmelinii can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used in essential oil form for its medicinal and insect-repelling properties.
